turns.content: Hey, I'm back at work after a five-year break and I'm trying to fix an error in my project. It's `error[E0512]: invalid type argument of 'Box' within a const context`. What went wrong?

Oh, that's a tricky one! That error often means your code is trying to use a feature introduced in Rust 1.46 (which added support for generic types inside `const` blocks), but you're compiling with an older version of the compiler. Have you checked the minimum supported Rust version in your Cargo.toml?
No, I didn't realize that was a thing. How do I know what's set there?

Easy! Your `Cargo.toml` has an `[package]` section where you can specify `edition = "2018"` or `edition = "2021"`, and the MSRV (Minimum Supported Rust Version) is often implied by that. But if you want to be explicit, there's a crate called [`cargo-msrv`](https://crates.io/crates/cargo-msrv). You can run `cargo msrv` in your terminal—it will tell you what the lowest version compatible with all dependencies is.
